Eight persons L, M, N, O, P, Q, R and S are sitting around a circular table such that some of them are facing inside while some are facing outside the table (but not necessarily in the same order). Not more than two adjacent persons face the same direction.
O sits second to the right of P where both are facing opposite directions. P sits opposite to R who sits second to the left of Q. Only two persons sit between L and Q when counted from the right of Q. Both L and R face the same direction. S faces inside. Only three persons sit between L and N who sits second to the right of S. Both M and S face opposite directions. The number of persons facing outside is two more than the number of persons facing inside. N doesn't face outside.
-

q: If all the persons are made to sit in the alphabetical order in a clockwise direction from L, then how many persons remain unchanged in their position (Including L)?

question: If in the number "386945275" all digits are arranged in descending order then what is the difference between the 3 rd digit from left end and 1 st digit from the right end?
q: If in the number "386945275" all digits are arranged in descending order then what is the difference between the 3rd digit from left end and 1st digit from the right end?
explanation: $Descending order: 987655432. 3 rd from left = 7. 1 st from right = 2. Difference = 7 × 2 = 5$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: $The speed of a boat in still water is 20 km/hr and the ratio of downstream to upstream speed of the boat is 5:3 respectively. Find the time taken by the boat to cover 240 km downstream (in hours)$.
q: $The speed of a boat in still water is 20 km/hr and the ratio of downstream to upstream speed of the boat is 5:3 respectively. Find the time taken by the boat to cover 240 km downstream (in hours)$.
explanation: $Still water speed = 4 x = 20 ⇒ x = 5. Downstream speed = 5 x = 25 km/h. Time = 240 ÷ 25 = 9.6 hours$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: A vessel contains milk and water in the ratio $5:3$, respectively. If 16 liters of the mixture are taken out and 4 liters of water are added, the ratio of milk to water becomes $3:2$. Find the initial quantity of milk (in liters).
q: A vessel contains milk and water in the ratio $5:3$, respectively. If 16 liters of the mixture are taken out and 4 liters of water are added, the ratio of milk to water becomes $3:2$. Find the initial quantity of milk (in liters).
explanation: $Let initial = 8 x. (5 x 10) ÷ (3 x 2) = \frac{3}{2} ⇒ x = 14. Initial milk = 5 x = 70 liters$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: $A and B together can complete a work in 20 days, and A alone can complete the same work in 30 days. If 40\% of the work is completed by B in (Y + 5) days, then find the value of Y$.
q: $A and B together can complete a work in 20 days, and A alone can complete the same work in 30 days. If 40\% of the work is completed by B in (Y + 5) days, then find the value of Y$.
explanation: $B's efficiency = \frac{1}{20} \frac{1}{30} = \frac{1}{60}. Time for 40\% work = 60 × 0.4 = 24 days ⇒ Y + 5 = 24 ⇒ Y = 19$.

q: Train X is 60% longer than Train Y. The speed of Train X is 90 km/h. Train X crosses a pole in 16 seconds. If both trains are running in the opposite direction and cross each other in 13 seconds, find the time taken by Train Y to cross a platform 500 meters long.
explanation: $Length \text{ of } X = 25 m/s 16 s = 400 m. Length \text{ of } Y = 250 m. Rel speed opposite = 650 ÷ 13 = 50 m/s ⇒ Speed \text{ of } Y = 25 m/s. Time to cross platform = (250+500)/25 = 30 seconds$.

question: A set M consists of five consecutive multiples of 6, while another set N consists of four consecutive multiples of 8. If the average of N is 4 less than the average of M, and the average of the smallest and largest numbers of M is 66, then find the $\sum$ of the numbers in set N.
q: A set M consists of five consecutive multiples of 6, while another set N consists of four consecutive multiples of 8. If the average of N is 4 less than the average of M, and the average of the smallest and largest numbers of M is 66, then find the sum of the numbers in set N.
explanation: $Average \text{ of } M = 66. Average \text{ of } N = 62. Sum \text{ of } N = 4 × 62 = 248$.

q: Riya scores 180 in English and 160 in Science. Meena scores 190 in English. If the average score of Meena is 10 more than the average score of Riya in both subjects, then find the average marks obtained by Riya and Meena in Science.
explanation: $Riya avg = 170. Meena avg = 180 ⇒ Meena sum = 360. Meena Science = 170. Avg Science score = (160 + 170)/2 = 165$.

q: Monthly saving of Ravi is 50% of his monthly expense. If his monthly expense increases by 50% and his monthly income increases by 20%, then his monthly saving decreases by Rs. 6000. Find the initial monthly income of Ravi.
explanation: $Let expense = 100 x, saving = 50 x, income = 150 x. New: expense=150 x, income=180 x, saving=30 x. Decrease = 20 x = 6000 ⇒ x = 300. Initial income = 150 300 = Rs 45000$.
